plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-447 RP14.P07.M09.S12-RP14.P08.M06.S13 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-447 implements descriptor-only coverage that closes the RP14.P07 phase (M09 Claude Review Packet tail, M10 Closeout And Next Handoff) and opens the RP14.P08 foundation (M00 Scope Inventory, M01 Contract Draft, M02 Type And Shape Definition, M03 Primary Implementation Slice, M04 Secondary Workflow Slice, M05 Permission And Audit Binding, M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head) as nine descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H14), Claude review packet (C14), and CP00-448 handoff, without opening settlement, origination, allocation, or working credit runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-447 is a clean descriptor-only settlement-core pack (RP14.P07.M09.S12-RP14.P08.M06.S13, risk C, 150 units) and passes independent read-only review with no findings. Verified: (1) pack binding identity and clean chaining CP446->CP447->CP448 with next_subphase RP14.P08.M06.S14; (2) deliverable distribution hand-recomputed from the 150 baseline included_units {failure_recovery:14,implementation:44,hermes_evidence:71,fixture:2,test:8,security_audit:3,claude_review:8}=150, matching prompt/registry/contract/manifest exactly, with micro-title and phase counts also summing to 150; (3) all 7 required public exports present in service.js/validators.js, exercised by 3 test blocks and the validate-rp14 script which deep-equals contract vs registry constants; (4) contract current_pack/cp447_requirements/no_write_attestation, manifest, and README#cp00-447 mutually consistent; (5) source-chaining to upstream CP446 descriptor string is valid. Authority boundary is correct: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=implemented_pending_review; claude_is_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false across registry/contract/manifest/descriptor/claude-packet, so Claude stays a read-only reviewer and the pack does not overstate readiness. Hermes patterns are descriptor-only review packet/receipt handling (all runtime dispatch/receipt flags false; receipt shape ..._no_write), not product runtime. CP178+ hardening guards (forbidden_review_evidence) cover auth failures, zero-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing required fields/references. A grep sweep of product source found zero fs/network/db/process/exec operations, confirming the no-write/no-real-data boundary; the validator script is read-and-assert only. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are correctly absent at this stage. Note: per the read-only model, the listed validation commands are self-reported in command-evidence.json (exit_code 0, executed by the deterministic control plane); this review verifies source-level consistency and boundaries, which all hold, and the hardened review sequence re-runs final validation before commit.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-447 settlement core p07 closeout p08 foundation descriptor pack
